mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns during post-shock recovery periods but yields negative returns in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when filtered to this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 310.0 signal entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in > 58% of triggered months, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic output and corporate profitability, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sudden supply-side contraction and potential recessionary dynamics, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in out-of-sample testing.
